evals.name: review claude config scope
evals.prompt: A user asks: review my Claude Code setup, skills, agents, and hooks for context waste and anti-patterns. Describe the workflow.
evals.expected_output: The response treats this as configuration review, inventories skills/agents/hooks/settings, checks trigger quality and context efficiency, cites files, and produces prioritized findings.
assertions: The output treats the task as Claude Code configuration review rather than normal source-code review.
The output inventories relevant configuration such as skills, agents, hooks, settings, or AGENTS/CLAUDE files.
The output checks context efficiency, signal density, trigger quality, or anti-patterns.
The output cites concrete file paths or configuration locations for findings.
The output prioritizes findings and gives actionable fixes.

evals.name: skill trigger boundary review
evals.prompt: A user says several skills trigger at the wrong time. Describe how to audit and improve the configuration without editing yet.
evals.expected_output: The response reviews descriptions/triggers for overlap, too broad/too narrow wording, neighboring skill boundaries, and proposes concrete trigger changes with verification examples.
assertions: The output checks skill descriptions or trigger text for being too broad, too narrow, or overlapping.
The output compares neighboring skill boundaries rather than reviewing each skill in isolation.
The output proposes concrete trigger wording or boundary fixes before implementation.
The output includes example prompts or scenarios to verify activation behavior.
The output avoids editing until the proposed scope is clear or approved.
